column1: # Pre-release PROMPT eval — golden set (TAB-separated; '#' lines ignored).
# columns:  question <TAB> must_contain <TAB> must_not_contain <TAB> manifest
#   must_contain / must_not_contain are COMMA-separated literal substrings checked
#   against the generated program (grep -F). This is the STABLE tier: it asserts the
#   model picks the right TOOLS / program SHAPE, which is far less flaky than scoring
#   the numeric answer. Each rule encodes a privacy_box-system.md HARD RULE or a
#   regression we fixed (the "$224,303 phone bill"). Shapes checked:
#     transactions(  — spending/count/total/biggest use the structured tool
#     money(         — amounts formatted with money(), not String(x)
#     manifest(      — vault-structure questions answer from the manifest
#     .tags / <tag>  — category spending filters transactions() on a KNOWN tag
#                      (the tag name appears in the filter), NOT ask_local/search
#     SUGGEST_TAG    — an UNKNOWN category proposes a tag (# SUGGEST_TAG: name : q),
#                      and must NOT force a wrong near-match (e.g. "health" for gym)
#     ask_local      — reading a value out of a DOCUMENT (a renewal date) calls the
#                      on-device reader; a meta count must NOT call it at all
#     ].id           — manifest files are VaultFile (field is `.alias`, not `.id`;
#                      `files[i].id` won't compile — the phone-bill regression)
#     search(        — wrong for exact transaction aggregates / meta questions
#     $" +           — manual dollar concat = the raw-float formatting bug
# The fixture tags (eval/fixtures/data/categories.txt) are the product defaults:
# phone, travel, restaurant, groceries, health. gym is deliberately NOT one → SUGGEST.
# question
how much did I pay for my phone bill
how much did I spend on groceries
how much did I spend on gym membership
how many transactions do I have
what is the total of my transactions
what was my biggest transaction
how many documents are in my vault
when does my car insurance renew
